title: Chapter 2 — The Verb Estar
description: Subject pronouns, the verb estar, adjectives of condition, and the four uses of estar

What does "estoy" mean?

What does "estás" mean?

What is the "ellos / ellas / ustedes" form of the verb "estar"?
questions.explanation: ellos / ellas / ustedes -> están (they/you all are)

questions.question: What does "están" mean?
Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Yo ______ en la clase."
questions.explanation: Yo estoy en la clase. = I am in the class.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Ella ______ enferma."
questions.explanation: Ella está enferma. = She is sick.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Nosotros ______ bien, gracias."
questions.explanation: Nosotros estamos bien, gracias. = We are fine, thanks.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "¿Cómo ______ tú?"
Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Ellos ______ cansados."
questions.explanation: Ellos están cansados. = They are tired.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "El restaurante ______ en la ciudad."
questions.explanation: El restaurante está en la ciudad. = The restaurant is in the city.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Vosotros ______ alegres."
questions.explanation: Vosotros estáis alegres. = You all are happy.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Las doctoras ______ enfermas."
questions.explanation: Las doctoras están enfermas. = The doctors are sick.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Yo ______ feliz hoy."
questions.explanation: Yo estoy feliz hoy. = I am happy today.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Nosotros ______ en el carro."
questions.explanation: Nosotros estamos en el carro. = We are in the car.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Ellas ______ en el baño."
questions.explanation: Ellas están en el baño. = They are in the bathroom.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "Él ______ guapo."
questions.explanation: Él está guapo. = He is handsome.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct form of "estar": "La sopa ______ sabrosa."
questions.explanation: La sopa está sabrosa. = The soup is delicious.

questions.question: Which subject pronoun goes with "estoy"?
questions.explanation: "estoy" is the yo (I) form of estar.

questions.question: Which subject pronoun goes with "estamos"?
questions.explanation: "estamos" is the nosotros (we) form of estar.

questions.question: Which subject pronoun goes with "están"?
questions.explanation: "están" is the ellos/ellas/ustedes form of estar.

Estar is used to express four basic concepts. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: Estar expresses: location, health, changing mood/condition, and personal opinion. Origin and nationality use "ser".

questions.question: "Yo estoy en la clase." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Yo estoy en la clase." (I am in the class.) uses estar for location.

questions.question: "El restaurante está en la ciudad." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "El restaurante está en la ciudad." (The restaurant is in the city.) uses estar for location.

questions.question: "Ellas están en el baño." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Ellas están en el baño." (They are in the bathroom.) uses estar for location.

questions.question: "Ella está enferma." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Ella está enferma." (She is sick.) uses estar for health.

questions.question: "Yo estoy bien, gracias." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Yo estoy bien, gracias." (I am fine, thanks.) uses estar for health.

questions.question: "Los doctores están enfermos." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Los doctores están enfermos." (The doctors are sick.) uses estar for health.

questions.question: "La muchacha está contenta." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "La muchacha está contenta." (The girl is happy.) uses estar for changing mood or condition.

questions.question: "Los hombres están cansados." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Los hombres están cansados." (The men are tired.) uses estar for changing mood or condition.

questions.question: "Estamos alegres." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Estamos alegres." (We are happy.) uses estar for changing mood or condition.

questions.question: "La comida está buena." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "La comida está buena." (The meal is good (tastes good).) uses estar for personal opinion.

questions.question: "El pescado está delicioso." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "El pescado está delicioso." (The fish is delicious.) uses estar for personal opinion.

questions.question: "Ella está hermosa hoy." — this uses estar to express…
questions.explanation: "Ella está hermosa hoy." (She is pretty today (looks pretty).) uses estar for personal opinion.

questions.question: Which verb is used to express temporary states, location, and changing conditions?
questions.explanation: "Estar" is used for location, health, mood, and personal opinion. "Ser" is used for permanent traits.

questions.question: "She is in the class." Which verb would you use in Spanish?
questions.explanation: Location uses "estar": "Ella esta en la clase."

questions.question: "He is a doctor." Which verb would you use in Spanish?
questions.explanation: Professions and permanent identity use "ser": "El es medico."

Translate to English: "Yo estoy bien, gracias."
questions.explanation: "Yo estoy bien, gracias." = "I am fine, thanks."

questions.question: Translate to English: "Ella está enferma."
Translate to English: "Los doctores están enfermos."
questions.explanation: "Los doctores están enfermos." = "The doctors are sick."

questions.question: Translate to English: "Los hombres están cansados."
questions.explanation: "Los hombres están cansados." = "The men are tired."

questions.question: Translate to English: "Estamos alegres."

questions.explanation: "El pescado está delicioso." = "The fish is delicious."

questions.explanation: "La sopa está sabrosa." = "The soup is delicious."

questions.explanation: "Ella está hermosa hoy." = "She is pretty today."

questions.question: Translate to English: "Él está guapo."
Translate to English: "Nosotros estamos en el carro."
questions.explanation: "Nosotros estamos en el carro." = "We are in the car."

questions.question: Translate to English: "El restaurante está en la ciudad."
questions.answer: The restaurant is in the city.
questions.explanation: "El restaurante está en la ciudad." = "The restaurant is in the city."

questions.question: Translate to English: "Ellas están en el baño."
questions.explanation: "Ellas están en el baño." = "They are in the bathroom."

questions.question: Translate to English: "¿Estás tú en el hospital?"

questions.question: Translate to English: "Estoy feliz."
Translate to Spanish: "I am fine, thanks."
questions.explanation: "I am fine, thanks." = "Yo estoy bien, gracias."

questions.question: Translate to Spanish: "She is sick."
Translate to Spanish: "We are in the car."
questions.explanation: "We are in the car." = "Nosotros estamos en el carro."

questions.question: Translate to Spanish: "The girl is happy."
questions.explanation: "The girl is happy." = "La muchacha está contenta."

questions.question: Translate to Spanish: "The men are tired."
questions.explanation: "The men are tired." = "Los hombres están cansados."

questions.question: Translate to Spanish: "Are you angry?"
Translate to Spanish: "The meal is good."
questions.explanation: "The meal is good." = "La comida está buena."
